extraction.one_line_summary: ALMEIDA João won a photo finish after Quintana hesitated on summit gravel descent at 2.7km to go, tied on time but Quintana takes GC lead on countback.
tactical_patterns: O'Connor attacked from yellow jersey to chase breakaway, rare defensive acceleration that backfired leaving him isolated and losing lead.
INEOS deployed Sivakov grind-down pace before Carapaz attacked 2.7km out, standard altitude attrition playbook forcing GC selection before summit.
Quintana lost stage and countback advantage by refusing to commit on summit gravel descent, tactical conservatism costing tangible result.
UAE placed Soler in break while Almeida sat in pack, dual-threat positioning yielded stage win when break absorbed.

rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: attacked from yellow jersey to chase break but got isolated, catastrophic energy expenditure defending race lead from front
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: won stage and took youth jersey while tied for GC, only UAE rider willing to descend aggressively on gravel
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: takes GC lead on countback but lost stage by hesitating on gravel, tactical caution undermining results
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: forced selection with 2.7km attack despite INEOS having Sivakov/Kwiatkowski/Porte present, leading from strength not numbers
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: explicitly dropped as off-color, illness impacting performance at altitude
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: positive: Colombian champion responded immediately to Carapaz attack and finished podium, elevation-specific strength emerging
